reasoning: The NFCIRISK subindex has exhibited strong short-term autoregressive trends. From its recent peak of -0.392 on April 18, 2025, it has declined monotonically over the past five weeks to -0.458 on May 23, dropping about ~0.015 per week. Given this significant and consistent recent downward momentum, it is highly unlikely to sharply reverse and exceed -0.458 by the very near-term date of June 1. For June 24, the downward cycle may be stabilizing but likely remains below the May 23 baseline. As we extend to August and November, the cyclical nature of the index (which recently took ~3 months to go from a trough of -0.51 in Jan to a peak of -0.39 in April) suggests historical mean-reversion could push it back up towards or above -0.458, increasing uncertainty toward a coin flip (0.50) for the 6-month horizon.
